purpose: Block B5 — single source of truth for cutover-gating item status. The CI test at tests/invariants/cutover-gate.test.ts reads this manifest and asserts every required item is verified=wired-and-exercised before runtime/cutover-flag.ts:CUTOVER_ENABLED can be set to true. Schema v2 adds the `verification` field after audit revealed many items were marked done but not actually exercised in production paths. `status` records whether the work was performed; `verification` records whether the work is doing its job in production. Block E readiness requires status=done AND verification=wired-and-exercised. Phase→Block lineage (the original routing refactor's Phases 0-7 are subsumed): Phase 0 → docs/ROUTING_STATE_MACHINE.md. Phase 1 → routing types + registry + codemod (DONE). Phase 2 → routing dispatcher + matrix tests (DONE, B5). Phase 3 → routing shadow mode (DONE). Phase 5 → v2 spec-edit wart fixes (DONE, FLAG-1..5). Phase 4 → Block E (cutover). Phase 6 → Block F1 (cleanup). Phase 7 → Block F2/F3 (Coder/Reviewer agents).

verification_definitions.burn-in: Work shipped; in legitimate burn-in window collecting real-traffic evidence.
verification_definitions.docs-only: Documentation written. Never exercised against real conditions (no drill, no real onboarding, no real incident).
verification_definitions.infrastructure-only: Code/module/script exists with tests, but is NOT WIRED into production callers, OR is narrower in scope than its title claims. Capability exists but is not yet doing its job.
verification_definitions.wired-and-exercised: Code is in production callers AND tested AND verified to fire by synthetic-violation testing. The capability is actively doing its job.

items.title: Fast-path decision recorded in rewrite map
runArchitectAgentV2 (all 7 branches)
items.verification_note: 100+ tests pass but the V2 runner is NOT wired into production — legacy runArchitectAgent still handles all real architect traffic. Only the shadow wrapper observes.

items.title: Architect escalation must consolidate ALL detected gaps (Bug B fix)
items.verification_note: Shipped: deterministic count helpers `countPlatformGapItems` and `countAgentGapItems` in `runtime/upstream-notice-format.ts` (Principle 11, no LLM in the gate path) + post-run consolidation gate in the architect path at `interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts`. When the architect calls `offer_upstream_revision(target=pm|design)` and the agent's question enumerates fewer items than `auditPmSpec`/`auditDesignSpec` detected, the gate overrides `pendingEscalation.question` with the consolidated platform brief from `parsePmGapText` / `parseDesignGapText`. Both PM and design targets covered per Principle 15 cross-agent parity. Log marker `[ESCALATION-GATE] B6:`. Tests: 8 unit tests for the count helpers in `tests/invariants/upstream-notice-contract.test.ts` + 5 regression tests (bug #13) in `tests/regression/architect-escalation-consolidation.test.ts` + 2 integration scenarios in `tests/integration/workflows.test.ts` (Scenario B6) covering positive (override fires when agent enumerates 1 of 3) and negative (faithful enumeration retained when agent matches platform count) cases. The integration scenario drives `handleFeatureChannelMessage` end-to-end through the architect tool_use path — `verification=wired-and-exercised` (exercised by integration tests, not just infrastructure-only). MT-20 is the optional spot-check during integration walks.

items.title: PM/Designer briefs must declare readOnly to the agent (Bug C fix)
items.verification_note: Shipped: shared constant runtime/readonly-brief-clause.ts (READONLY_AGENT_BRIEF_CLAUSE) declares the no-spec-writing-tools contract — names the canonical action-claim phrasings the agent must avoid ('Applying the patch...', 'I'll update the spec...') and names the resolution (human says yes, platform applies). Constant injected via template interpolation at all 4 readOnly brief sites in interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts: designer→PM (line 590), designer→architect (line 605), architect→designer (line 943), architect→PM (line 959). Per Principle 15 cross-agent parity. Tests: structural invariant tests/invariants/readonly-brief-clause.test.ts (9 tests) pins both halves of the contract — the constant exists with the right semantics (4 module-level tests) AND every brief site interpolates it (5 cross-agent tests including a heading-proximity defense-in-depth check that catches new readOnly call sites lacking a brief) + 5 regression tests (bug #15) in tests/regression/readonly-brief-clause.test.ts. Verification stays infrastructure-only until MT-22 confirms agents honor the clause in real Slack — the fix is prompt-rule (probabilistic per Principle 8); structural invariant ensures the clause is always PRESENT, but not always OBEYED by the LLM. If real-Slack non-compliance recurs, v2 will add a runtime stripper (same architectural pattern as Block N2's tool-name sentence-drop) targeting action-claim verb phrases in readOnly responses.

items.title: Engineering-spec writeback must respect SSOT (Bug D fix)
items.verification_note: Shipped: codified CLAUDE.md Principle 16 (Spec write ownership — resolved decisions land only in the owner's spec; carve-outs for preseed-open-items / handoff sections explicitly documented) plus per-agent owner table. Removed patchEngineeringSpecWithDecision call from architect's upstream-revision-reply branch in interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts (the violation: PM/designer-authored content was being recorded under `### Architect Decision (pre-engineering)` heading in the architect-owned engineering spec, append-only layout producing N duplicate-heading blocks per N escalations). Single legitimate call remains (Path A: designer→architect escalation reply, architect-authored content → engineering spec). Architect re-reads upstream spec on every run via loadArchitectAgentContext, so no information is lost. Cross-agent audit confirmed exactly one violation existed system-wide; all other writebacks (saveDraft*, saveApproved*, patch*Spec*, preseed*, seedHandoffSection, updateApprovedSpecOnMain) are owner-consistent or fall in documented carve-outs. Tests: structural invariant tests/invariants/spec-write-ownership.test.ts (19 tests — AST-greps every callsite and pins to documented allow-list; adding a new writeback requires updating manifest in same commit) + 4 regression tests in tests/regression/spec-write-ownership.test.ts (bug #14) + flipped existing integration Scenario N44b (was pinning the buggy behavior; now asserts engineering spec NOT touched and product spec IS written). MT-21 spot-check tier (integration coverage end-to-end). AGENTS.md gained per-agent owner table under 'Spec write ownership' subsection.

items.title: patchProductSpecWithRecommendations must apply category rules deterministically (Bug E fix) — both PM and design escalation paths (Principle 15 cross-agent parity)
items.verification_note: Shipped: deterministic runtime/category-rule-extractor.ts (pure, no LLM, @deterministic per Principle 11) exports extractCategoryRules / applyCategoryRules / findResidualCategoryViolations. Parses universal-quantified substitution patterns — any/all/every 'X' becomes/should be/→/-> 'Y'; replace [all] 'X' with/by 'Y'; change every 'X' to 'Y'. Conservative on quantifier — single-criterion edits without universal cue are NOT treated as category rules. Word-boundary case-insensitive substitution. Wired into BOTH escalation-spec writers (Principle 15 cross-agent parity): runtime/pm-escalation-spec-writer.ts (PM recommendations → product spec) AND runtime/design-escalation-spec-writer.ts (Designer recommendations → design spec). Same shared module — single source of truth for the parser. Pre-Haiku application + post-Haiku residual safety net (re-applies if Haiku's merge re-introduced any from-word). Log markers `[ESCALATION] B9: extracted N category rule(s)` and `[ESCALATION] B9: Haiku's merge re-introduced N category-rule term(s)`. Tests: 25 unit tests + 9 regression tests (bug #16, including 3 cross-agent-parity assertions verifying both writers are wired) + 2 integration scenarios — Scenario B9 (architect→PM via designer→PM escalation, deliberately-buggy mocked Haiku patch replaces 2 of 4 instances) and Scenario B9b (architect→designer escalation, same shape, replaces 1 of 3 instances). Both scenarios assert the saved spec has 0 surviving from-words, proving the residual safety net is the structural guarantee on both paths. MT-23 spot-check tier covers both paths. CODE_MARKER bump for the cross-agent ship: b9b-design-category-rule-cross-agent-parity. verification=wired-and-exercised.

items.title: Platform-built messages must not impersonate the agent in third person (Bug F fix)
items.verification_note: Shipped: shared constant runtime/platform-message-prefix.ts (PLATFORM_MESSAGE_PREFIX = '*Platform —*') used as the prefix for every platform-composed Slack notification. Cross-agent audit confirmed exactly 6 agent-name static prefix sites in interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts (all in the same file, no other handlers); all 6 converted to use the shared constant + bodies rewritten in platform first-person voice ('we'll bring the PM agent back into this thread' instead of bare 'Say *yes* to bring the PM back' imperative). Structural invariant tests/invariants/platform-message-prefix.test.ts (10 tests) AST-greps every handler file's `text:` template literals and fails on any agent-name static prefix from FORBIDDEN_AGENT_PREFIXES; only ${mention} (Slack <@U…> ping or text-fallback role label addressing the human role-holder) is allowed. Tests: 5 regression tests (bug #17) in tests/regression/platform-message-prefix.test.ts including a structural assertion that no agent-name static prefix survives + integration scenario B10 in tests/integration/workflows.test.ts that drives handleFeatureChannelMessage through a re-escalation flow and asserts the actual posted message starts with '*Platform —*' and no posted message uses an agent-name prefix. Manifest verification=wired-and-exercised. MT-24 spot-check tier (3 layers of automated coverage end-to-end).

items.title: Spec-content verifier — no agent recommendation passes without verifying cited content exists (architectural gap; covers Bug G)
items.verification_note: v1 shipped: runtime/spec-content-verifier.ts (pure deterministic, @deterministic per Principle 11) exports extractAcMap, findAcReferences, extractQuotedPhrasesNear, verifyAcReferences, formatHallucinations. Detects two classes: ac-does-not-exist (cited AC# beyond spec count) and claimed-wording-not-in-ac (quoted phrase not in cited AC body). Wired log-only at PM escalation-resume site (interfaces/slack/handlers/message.ts, both arch-upstream-escalation-confirmed and arch-upstream-continuation branches) — emits [CONTENT-VERIFIER] log line on detection. 28 unit tests + 5 regression tests (bug #12, tests/regression/spec-content-hallucination-detection.test.ts) cover happy path, both hallucination classes, determinism invariant, and structural wiring assertion. v2 work (re-prompt loop + downstream patcher gating + cross-agent extension to designer + architect + brand-token / screen-name / section-heading verifiers) tracked separately. MT-19 is the real-Slack scenario; verification stays infrastructure-only until MT-19 passes.

items.title: PM hallucinates AC numbers / fabricates spec content (Bug G fix — subsumed by B11)
items.verification_note: Subsumed by B11 v1 — the AC-citation hallucination class (both ac-does-not-exist and claimed-wording-not-in-ac) is now detected at the PM escalation-resume site via runtime/spec-content-verifier.ts. v1 is log-only — operator sees [CONTENT-VERIFIER] in logs and intervenes before the spec is corrupted. v2 (re-prompt loop + patcher gating) will fully retire the user-as-line-of-defense pattern. Regression catalog bug #12. MT-19 is the real-Slack scenario.

items.title: Multi-tenant correctness verification (sandbox tenant-B parallel run)
Pre-cutover migration smoke against prod state

Real-fixture producer tests for every classifier
Error-path coverage (GitHub 502, Slack rate-limit, Anthropic 529, GitHub 401)
State-corruption recovery (partial write, restart race, unknown fields)
Chaos / fuzzing (message ordering, mid-turn restart, concurrent spec edits)
Escalation notifications must survive bot restart within TTL (D5 fix, was Bug A)
items.verification_note: Fixed 2026-04-30 in commit (this work). EscalationNotification type now carries `timestamp` (set by setEscalationNotification). Startup uses clearStaleEntries(escalationNotifications, 'escalationNotification') with PENDING_STATE_TTL_MS (24h). 4 unit tests in tests/regression/escalation-notification-survives-restart.test.ts verify the type round-trip, timestamp stamping, and structural absence of the legacy clear-all pattern. Verification grade is `infrastructure-only` (not `wired-and-exercised`) until MT-18 runs in real Slack against a real restart — promotes to wired-and-exercised after that. Regression catalog bug #11. Resolves J3↔D5 collision.

items.title: Log-coverage gate (every state mutation has structured log line)
items.verification_note: Invariant scans only runtime/conversation-store.ts mutators. Misses message.ts post-run mutations (escalation override replacement, hedge-gate skip path), tool-handler state mutations, github-client state writes. The G1 spirit demands platform-wide; the implementation is one file.
